factual-accuracy.requirement: States M1 applications open February 13, 2026 and close April 3, 2026 for the Master's in Economics program at Université Paris-Saclay
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response does not state that M1 applications open on February 13, 2026 and close on April 3, 2026. Instead, it gives different dates for M1 Economics via INCEPTION (January 15, 2026 to March 16, 2026), so the required opening/closing dates are absent.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States M2 applications open February 25, 2026 and close May 25, 2026 for the Master's in Economics program at Université Paris-Saclay
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly states under the M2 Economics deadlines that applications open on February 25, 2026 and close on May 25, 2026.

factual-accuracy.requirement: Lists Bachelor's in Economics (BA Economics/Licence d'Economie) or Mathematics (BS Mathematics/Licence de Mathématique) as required academic background for M1 and M2 candidates
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly states that applications for both M1 and M2 are open to students with a BA in economics or a BS in mathematics, matching the required academic background criterion.

factual-accuracy.requirement: Notes that applicants who hold a degrees in sciences and social sciences from a top university will still be considered for the Master's in Economics program at Université Paris-Saclay
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly notes that, beyond a BA in economics or BS in mathematics, “other degrees in sciences and social sciences from best universities will be considered” for admission to the Master of Economics at Université Paris-Saclay.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States that the Master's in Economics program at Université Paris-Saclay is taught in English
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly states in the Program Overview that the Master of Economics at Université Paris-Saclay is “taught entirely in English.”

factual-accuracy.requirement: States that M1 and M2 candidates are required to submit a curriculum vitae (CV), a motivation letter, all validated transcripts since high school diploma, a copy of an identity document, and a motivation letter
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response lists required documents for the M1 Economics application including a motivation letter, CV, validated transcripts since high school diploma, and an identity document. However, it does not state that M1 and M2 candidates are required to submit these items, nor does it include the duplicated requirement of a second motivation letter.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States that M1 candidates are required to submit copy diplomas
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response lists “Diplomas/Certificates” among required documents and notes they are “of the highest obtained degree,” but it does not state that M1 candidates are required to submit copy diplomas specifically. factual-accuracy.requirement: States that M2 candidates are required to submit either a letter of recommendation or an internship evaluation
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response states that “Recommendation letters or internship evaluations” are required and explains that internship evaluations from professional supervisors can substitute for recommendation letters, which implies M2 candidates must submit either a letter of recommendation or an internship evaluation.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States that M1 and M2 candidates who are non-native English speakers are required to either submit a certificate of English level or their GMAT/GRE test results
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response says GRE/GMAT scores are not required and only optionally submitted, and it does not state that non-native English-speaking M1/M2 candidates are required to submit either an English certificate or GMAT/GRE results. It mentions “proof of English proficiency or GMAT/GRE scores” but frames GMAT/GRE as optional rather than a required alternative.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States that M1 and M2 candidates who have already been enrolled in higher education in France before are required to submit two letters of recommendation
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response discusses a two-referee rule based on whether applicants were enrolled in French higher education in the last two academic years, and even suggests those who studied in France may not need referees. It does not state that M1 and M2 candidates previously enrolled in higher education in France are required to submit two letters of recommendation.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States that M1 and M2 candidates requesting a valuation of assets to enter the diploma are required to submit a VAP file
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response mentions a VAPP dossier for recognition of professional experience but does not state that M1 and M2 candidates requesting a valuation of assets to enter the diploma are required to submit a VAP file. It contains no requirement tying M1/M2 entry via asset valuation to submission of a VAP/VAP file.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States Charpak is for Indian citizens or OCI card holders
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly states under Charpak eligibility (B1.1 Nationality) that applicants must be an “Indian citizen or hold an OCI card,” indicating the scholarship is for Indian citizens or OCI card holders.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States Charpak applicants must be be 30 years old or younger at the time of application
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly states under “B1.2. Age Limit — Strict 30 Years Maximum” that applicants “must be 30 years old or younger at the time of application.”

factual-accuracy.requirement: States that Charpak is only available for full-time master's degree programs
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response describes Charpak as being for “full-time, in-person Master’s degree studies” and notes part-time/distance learning are not eligible, but it does not explicitly state that Charpak is only available for full-time master’s degree programs as a general availability rule.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States PhD students are NOT eligible for Charpak
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly lists “PhD students” under “Summary of Ineligibility Conditions” for the Charpak Master’s Scholarship as “NOT ELIGIBLE,” stating that PhD students are not eligible for Charpak.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States previous Charpak scholarship recipients are NOT eligible to apply again
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly lists “Previous Charpak scholarship recipients” under “Summary of Ineligibility Conditions” as “NOT ELIGIBLE,” which states that prior recipients cannot apply again.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States that students participating in semester exchange programs, internships, or research projects are not eligible to apply for Charpak

factual-accuracy.requirement: States that students already pursuing a master's in France can apply for Charpak for their second year (M2)
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly states under section B1.3 (“Already in France”) that Indian students already pursuing a master’s in France may apply for Charpak funding for their second year only, which corresponds to eligibility for M2.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States that Charpak will not cover any part of the course that does not take place in France, such as a study-abroad semester
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response states that Charpak is for full-time Master’s studies conducted entirely in France and explicitly lists “programs conducted partially outside France” as not eligible, implying it will not cover any course component outside France (e.g., a study-abroad semester).

factual-accuracy.requirement: States knowledge of French is an asset, but not mandatory for Charpak applicants
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response notes that a French language certificate is optional for Charpak applicants (“Optional but recommended”) and that French is not required, implying it is an asset but not mandatory.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States that preference for Charpak will be given to applicants who have completed their education at an Indian institution, but applicants with a degree from a non-Indian institution are still eligible
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response says applicants must be enrolled in or have completed studies at an Indian institution and notes “preference is given” to candidates with an Indian degree, while also indicating Indian students studying abroad/with foreign degrees are not explicitly excluded but are disadvantaged.

factual-accuracy.requirement: Provides https://www.inde.campusfrance.org/charpak-master-s-scholarship as application portal URL
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response includes the URL https://www.inde.campusfrance.org/charpak-master-s-scholarship multiple times, including in the sources list as the Charpak Master’s Scholarship page.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States formal passport-size photograph required for Charpak applicants
factual-accuracy.explanation: In the Charpak scholarship required documents list, the response explicitly includes a “Passport-size Photograph” as mandatory and later reiterates that a “formal photograph” is required in the portal uploads section.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States copy of the first page of their passport (with photo and expiry date) required for Charpak applicants
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response lists “Copy of Valid Passport” as a required Charpak document but does not specify that a copy of the first page (showing photo and expiry date) is required. factual-accuracy.requirement: States Statement of Purpose required for Charpak applicants
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly lists “Statement of Purpose” as a mandatory required document for the France Excellence Charpak Master’s Scholarship in section B2.1 and further details the SOP requirements in section B2.3.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States one to two page curriculum vitae (CV) required for Charpak applicants

factual-accuracy.requirement: States admission/acceptance letter from French higher education institution required for Charpak applicants
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly lists an “Admission Letter” as a mandatory Charpak document and specifies it must be from a French institution (i.e., a French higher education institution).

factual-accuracy.requirement: States copies of mark sheets and degrees from Higher Secondary School (XII), Bachelor's, and Master's (including those of completed semesters of the ongoing degree) required for Charpak applicants

factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly states that Charpak requires “Letter(s) of recommendation from the current Indian university/employer or the institution last attended,” indicating recommendation letters from a current Indian university or employer are required for applicants.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States Charpak deadline was February 28, 2025, 23:59 IST for the 2025 cycle
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response gives a Charpak deadline of March 30, 2026 (23:59 IST) for the 2026 cycle and does not mention February 28, 2025, 23:59 IST for the 2025 cycle. The specific required deadline statement for the 2025 cycle is absent.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States shortlisted Charpak applicants were interviewed online between April 7, 2025 and April 25, 2025
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response states that online interviews for shortlisted Charpak candidates occurred between April 13 and April 30, 2026. It does not mention interviews being held between April 7, 2025 and April 25, 2025, so the specified date range is absent.

factual-accuracy.explanation: The response states under section B3.4 that “Multiple applications from the same candidate will be rejected — only one application per person,” which conveys that only one online application is allowed per Charpak candidate.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: Explains Charpak uses two-stage selection process (document review followed by online interviews) with specific evaluation criteria
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response describes a two-stage Charpak selection process: an initial document evaluation/screening followed by online interviews for shortlisted candidates. breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: Explains Charpak Statement of Purpose is screened for plagiarism, and any application found with significant AI-generated content will be disqualified
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response explicitly states that the Charpak Statement of Purpose will undergo a plagiarism check and that applications with significant AI-generated content will be disqualified, in the “Anti-AI / Anti-plagiarism policy” subsection.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: States that Charpak Scholarship winners will be awarded monthly living allowance of 860 euros, exemption of visa if applying from India, student social security and additional health insurance, assistance in finding affordable accommodations, and meal discounts at university restaurants
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response’s “Scholarship Benefits” section states a €860/month living allowance, visa fee exemption for student visa applications, inclusion of student social security and additional health insurance (mutuelle), housing assistance in finding affordable accommodation, and meal discounts at university restaurants.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: Identifies alternative funding options such as Eiffel Excellence Scholarship for those ineligible to apply for Charpak
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response includes a dedicated “Alternative Funding Pathways” section that identifies the France Excellence Eiffel Scholarship as an alternative and explains its eligibility and application process, positioning it as an option when Charpak is not available/compatible.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: Notes application procedures vary by nationality with recommendation to verify procedures annually on program-specific pages
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response explicitly notes that application procedures depend on nationality (e.g., Indian nationals must use INCEPTION plus Études en France, while others may use Mon Master) and advises monitoring official program pages regularly for updated deadlines and requirements, implying annual verification on program-specific pages.

breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.requirement: Recommends completing Charpak scholarship application before applying to or receiving acknowledgment from the French university program
breadth-and-depth-of-analysis.explanation: The response states that applicants must have applied to a French institution prior to applying for the Charpak scholarship and that an admission letter or proof of application is required. It does not recommend completing the Charpak application before applying to or receiving acknowledgment from the French university program.

presentation-quality.requirement: Maintains professional academic guidance tone without promotional language or casual phrasing
presentation-quality.explanation: The response uses a formal, structured report style with neutral academic wording and avoids overt marketing or sales language. While it includes emphatic formatting (e.g., “CRITICAL,” “IMPORTANT”), it remains guidance-oriented rather than casual or promotional.

presentation-quality.requirement: Uses hierarchical organization with clear sections separating program admission from scholarship information
presentation-quality.explanation: The response is clearly split into labeled sections, with “Part A” dedicated to Paris-Saclay Master’s admission requirements and “Part B” dedicated to the France Excellence Charpak scholarship eligibility/application requirements, keeping the two topics separated in a hierarchical structure.

presentation-quality.requirement: Formats requirements, documents, and procedures as lists or bullet points for clarity
presentation-quality.explanation: The response consistently formats admission requirements, eligibility criteria, required documents, procedures, and deadlines using bullet lists and multiple structured tables (e.g., document checklists, eligibility matrices, and step-by-step processes).

presentation-quality.requirement: Restates the same eligibility criterion, deadline, or document requirement multiple times without adding new context
presentation-quality.explanation: The response repeats certain requirements and deadlines multiple times (e.g., Charpak non-cumulation rules and the March 30, 2026 deadline) across sections and tables, often restating the same criterion without adding materially new context.

presentation-quality.requirement: Uses excessive wordiness, filler phrases, or unnecessary elaboration that extends length without adding substantive information
presentation-quality.explanation: The response is extremely long and includes extensive tangential sections (e.g., tuition-fee decree analysis, alternative scholarships, exhaustive combination matrices, and detailed course lists) beyond what the query asks. It also uses filler framing like “Comprehensive Research Report,” “CRITICAL,” and repeated procedural elaborations that add length without directly advancing the requested Nov 2025 requirements summary.

citation-quality.requirement: Cites Université Paris-Saclay official Master in Economics program pages (such as https://www.universite-paris-saclay.fr/en/education/master/economics) for admission requirements, deadlines, or documents
citation-quality.explanation: The response cites official Université Paris-Saclay Master in Economics program pages (e.g., the M1 and M2 Economics pages on universite-paris-saclay.fr) and uses them to support statements about admission requirements, required documents, and application deadlines.

citation-quality.requirement: References Campus France India website (campusfrance.org, https://www.inde.campusfrance.org/charpak-master-s-scholarship) for Charpak scholarship eligibility, benefits, or application process
citation-quality.explanation: The response explicitly cites and links to the Campus France India Charpak Master’s Scholarship page (https://www.inde.campusfrance.org/charpak-master-s-scholarship) and uses it as a source for eligibility, benefits, and application requirements.

citation-quality.requirement: Cites Reddit, Quora, student blogs, or third-party scholarship aggregators as primary sources for eligibility criteria or deadlines
citation-quality.explanation: The response does not cite Reddit, Quora, student blogs, or third-party scholarship aggregators as primary sources for eligibility criteria or deadlines. Its citations are mainly official university/government pages and some news/other informational sites (e.g., The Local, ICEF, GyanDhan), but none of the prohibited platforms.
